attractions.description: The Adele H. Stamp Student Union, commonly known as 'Stamp,' is the vibrant heart of campus life at the University of Maryland, College Park. Spanning over 300,000 square feet, it serves as a central hub for student organizations, recreation, dining, and cultural activities, welcoming more than 10,000 visitors daily. attractions.name: Herbert W. Wells Ice Rink
attractions.description: Herbert W. Wells Ice Rink is a semi-enclosed, open-air ice facility featuring a roof and a standard NHL-sized skating surface. Operated by the Maryland-National Capital Park and Planning Commission (M-NCPPC), it provides a unique outdoor atmosphere for seasonal skating from October through April. The facility includes a warming room, locker rooms with showers, and a party room available for private rentals. Official Site, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Jim Henson Statue and Memorial Garden
attractions.description: The Jim Henson Statue and Memorial Garden is a bronze tribute to University of Maryland alumnus and Muppets creator Jim Henson. Dedicated on September 24, 2003, it features a 450-pound statue of Henson conversing with Kermit the Frog on a red granite bench, surrounded by a landscaped garden of crape myrtles and shrub roses. (University of Maryland, UMD Arboretum)

For 47 years, it served as the thunderous home of Terrapin basketball and hosted legendary events like the 1966 Final Four and 'Ping-Pong Diplomacy.' Today, it has been renovated into a premier football training facility and research hub while preserving its iconic historic facade. University of Maryland Athletics, Jones-Hill House Wikipedia

attractions.description: The Kibel and Linear Galleries are the primary exhibition spaces for the University of Maryland's School of Architecture, Planning and Preservation. The Kibel Gallery showcases professional research and creative work focused on the built environment, while the Linear Gallery serves as a student-directed forum for cross-disciplinary collaboration and student achievements. Together, they provide a dynamic platform for exploring architecture, urban planning, and historic preservation through rotating exhibitions.

attractions.name: Lake Artemesia Natural Area at Lakeland
attractions.description: Lake Artemesia is a 38-acre man-made lake and natural area that serves as a premier destination for bird watching and outdoor recreation. The park features a 1.35-mile paved loop trail, aquatic gardens, and several fishing piers, all of which are wheelchair accessible. It was created in the late 1980s as a mitigation project during the construction of the Washington Metro Green Line, repurposing a former sand and gravel excavation site. (Park and Recreation - Prince George's County, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Lincoln Memorial
attractions.description: The Lincoln Memorial is a U.S. national memorial honoring Abraham Lincoln, the 16th president of the United States. Located on the western end of the National Mall, it is designed in the style of a neoclassical Greek temple and houses a colossal 19-foot marble statue of a seated Lincoln. (Wikipedia, NPS)
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours a day; Park Rangers are on duty from 9:30 a.m. to 10:00 p.m. daily to answer questions. (NPS, Washington.org)
attractions.admission: Free (The memorial is free to the public; upcoming museum in 2026 may require timed tickets but is expected to be free) (NPS, Hotel Washington)
highlights: 'The 19-foot-tall marble statue of Abraham Lincoln sculpted by Daniel Chester French. [NPS', "Inscriptions of Lincoln's famous speeches, the Gettysburg Address and the Second Inaugural Address. NPS", "The 36 fluted Doric columns representing the states in the Union at the time of Lincoln's death. NPS", 'The Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool, which offers iconic views of the Washington Monument and Capitol. Wikipedia', 'Historical significance as the site of Martin Luther King Jr.\'s "I Have a Dream" speech. Washington.org', "The upcoming Undercroft Museum (opening 2026) showcasing the memorial's construction and history. Hotel Washington"]
attractions.tips: Visit during early morning or late evening for a more tranquil experience and to avoid crowds. Use public transportation (Metro stations Foggy Bottom or Smithsonian) as parking is very limited. Bring water and sunscreen for summer visits, and wear comfortable walking shoes for the National Mall. (NPS, Old Town Trolley)

Known for its lively atmosphere, it features a spacious bar area, a dining room, and a large outdoor patio, making it a popular hub for students, alumni, and local residents. The venue offers a wide selection of pub fare, including Maryland-style crab dishes and wings, alongside live entertainment and numerous screens for sports viewing.

attractions.name: McKeldin Mall
attractions.description: McKeldin Mall is a stunning nine-acre quad located in the heart of the University of Maryland, College Park campus. As the largest academic mall in the United States, it serves as a central hub for student life, academic ceremonies, and recreation. The mall is famously anchored by the McKeldin Library and the Main Administration Building, and it is widely recognized as one of the most picturesque campus greens in the country.

Standing at over 555 feet tall, it remains the world's tallest stone structure and the world's tallest obelisk (National Park Service). The monument serves as a central landmark on the National Mall, offering panoramic views of the nation's capital from its observation deck (National Park Service).

attractions.tips: Advance online tickets are highly recommended as they often sell out 30 days in advance (National Park Service). Large bags, strollers, and weapons are strictly prohibited (National Park Service).

Frederick Gallery features rotating contemporary exhibitions (Official Site)', 'Offers professional-grade community studios for rent to certified artists (Official Site)', 'Hosts annual events such as the Member MashUp and the 10x10 Invitational (Official Site)', 'Provides a wide range of educational workshops and custom one-on-one instruction (Official Site)', 'Located in a historic 13,000 square foot facility within the Gateway Arts District (Yelp)']
attractions.tips: Comfortable, close-toed shoes and short sleeves are recommended for studio work, and long hair should be tied back for safety (Official FAQ). Public transit users can reach the center via the Green Line (West Hyattsville Station + P33 bus) or Red Line (Rhode Island Ave-Brentwood Station + P10 bus) (Official FAQ).

attractions.name: The National Archives at College Park, Maryland
attractions.description: The National Archives at College Park, also known as Archives II, is a major facility of the United States National Archives and Records Administration. It holds a vast array of permanent Federal records, including textual documents, photographs, and electronic records. The facility serves as a primary hub for historians and researchers looking to access historical government data and specialized media collections (National Archives).
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Saturday: Closed, Sunday: Closed (National Archives)
attractions.admission: Free (National Archives)
highlights: 'John F. Kennedy Assassination Records ([National Archives)', 'World War I and Vietnam War Records (National Archives)', 'Still Pictures and Electronic Records (National Archives)', 'Cartographic and Architectural Holdings (National Archives)', 'Motion Picture, Sound, and Video Records (National Archives)', 'Berlin Documents Center Microfilm (National Archives)']
attractions.tips: Research appointments are highly encouraged, although walk-ins are permitted. Visitors should review research room policies and bring a valid photo ID for researcher registration before arriving (National Archives).
attractions.nearby: University of Maryland, College Park Aviation Museum, Lake Artemesia Park, Paint Branch Trail (City of College Park)

attractions.name: United States National Agricultural Library
attractions.description: The National Agricultural Library (NAL) is one of five national libraries of the United States and the largest agricultural library in the world (National Agricultural Library). Established in 1862, it serves as the primary source for agricultural information and research, housing millions of volumes in its iconic 15-story Abraham Lincoln Building (Experience Prince George's).
attractions.address: 10301 Baltimore Avenue, Beltsville, MD 20705 (National Agricultural Library)
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 8:30 AM – 4:30 PM; Special Collections: 8:30 AM – 12:00 PM and 1:00 PM – 4:00 PM (National Agricultural Library)
attractions.admission: Free (YouTube)
highlights: "Houses one of the world's largest collections devoted to agriculture and related sciences ([National Agricultural Library)", 'Home to the 15-story Abraham Lincoln Building, a recognizable landmark in the landscape (Agricultural Research Service)', 'Features extensive Special Collections, including historic posters and over 200,000 seed trade catalogs (National Agricultural Library)', 'Coordinator for the Agriculture Network Information Center (AgNIC) and a primary research arm for the USDA (Wikipedia)', 'Located on the vast 6,600-acre grounds of the Henry A. Wallace Beltsville Agricultural Research Center (Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: Visitors should first obtain a visitor badge at the security desk in Building 003 at 10300 Baltimore Avenue (Agricultural Research Service). The library lobby features beautiful Chinese viewing stones and rotating exhibits that are open to the public (YouTube). The building is fully wheelchair accessible with designated parking available (National Agricultural Library).
attractions.nearby: Beltsville Agricultural Research Center, NASA Goddard Space Flight Center, University of Maryland, Greenbelt Park (Wikipedia, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: University of Maryland Golf Course
attractions.description: The University of Maryland Golf Course is a premier 18-hole championship-style venue and a hub for the university's recreational and educational community (University of Maryland Golf Course). As a certified Audubon Cooperative Sanctuary, it serves as a treasured greenspace and living classroom while providing a challenging experience for golfers of all skill levels (University of Maryland Golf Course).
attractions.address: 3800 Golf Course Road, College Park, MD 20742
attractions.hours: Driving Range Hours: Mon 10am-8:00pm; Tue-Thu 7am-8:00pm; Fri 7am-7:00pm; Sat 6:30am-8:00pm; Sun 6:30am-4:00pm (Tee times vary by season) (University of Maryland Golf Course)
attractions.admission: Guest Rates (Spring 2026): Mon-Thurs $52-$75, Fri $80, Sat/Sun/Holidays $85 (includes riding); Student Rates: $35-$65; Driving Range: $3-$14 per bucket (University of Maryland Golf Course)
highlights: '18-hole championship-style course with a detailed digital tour ([University of Maryland Golf Course)', 'Full-service driving range with both grass and mat hitting areas (University of Maryland Golf Course)', 'On-site dining available at Mulligan’s Grill & Pub (University of Maryland Golf Course)', 'Certified Audubon Cooperative Sanctuary highlighting its commitment to environmental stewardship (University of Maryland Golf Course)', 'PGA professional instruction and player development programs (University of Maryland Golf Course)']
attractions.tips: The facility is strictly cashless, so bring a credit card for all payments (University of Maryland Golf Course). Ensure you arrive at least 20 minutes before your scheduled tee time and adhere to the required golf attire, including collared shirts (University of Maryland Golf Course). Outside food and alcohol are not permitted on the premises (University of Maryland Golf Course).

attractions.name: Albert S. "Buddy" Attick Lake Park (Greenbelt MD)
attractions.description: Buddy Attick Lake Park is a scenic community space centered around a picturesque 23-acre lake in the historic planned city of Greenbelt. The park features a 1.25-mile wooded trail perfect for hiking and biking, along with amenities such as picnic pavilions, playgrounds, and opportunities for fishing and seasonal kayaking. (Greenbelt MD, Birders Guide)
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 5:00 AM – 11:00 PM (Greenbelt MD)
attractions.admission: Free (Greenbelt MD, Birders Guide)
highlights: 'Picturesque 23-acre lake centerpiece for recreation and wildlife viewing ([Greenbelt MD)', '1.25-mile stone and dirt path circling the lake for walkers and bikers (Greenbelt MD)', 'Picnic pavilions equipped with grills, tables, and benches (Greenbelt MD)', 'Playground and tot lot facilities for children (Greenbelt MD)', 'Diverse birdlife with over 190 species reported, including seasonal waterfowl (Birders Guide)', 'Pollinator garden located near the park entrance (Greenbelt MD)']
attractions.tips: Visit in the early morning or on weekdays to avoid crowds; restrooms typically close at dusk. While canoeing and kayaking are allowed, they require permits which are issued only to Greenbelt residents. (Birders Guide, Greenbelt MD)
attractions.nearby: Greenbelt National Park, Greenbelt Aquatic & Fitness Center, University of Maryland (College Park), Lake Artemesia (Greenbelt MD, Expedia)

attractions.name: Arlington National Cemetery
attractions.description: Arlington National Cemetery is the largest military cemetery in the United States, serving as the final resting place for more than 400,000 service members and their families. Established during the Civil War on the grounds of Arlington House, it remains an active burial ground and a hallowed site for honoring the nation's veterans (Wikipedia, Arlington National Cemetery Official Site).
attractions.hours: Daily, 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. (Arlington National Cemetery Official Site)
attractions.admission: Free for all visitors to walk and tour the grounds (Arlington National Cemetery Official Site, Washington.org). Interpretive tram tours are available for a fee (Arlington Tours).
highlights: 'Tomb of the Unknown Soldier and the Changing of the Guard ceremony ([Official Site)', 'John F. Kennedy Gravesite featuring the Eternal Flame (Official Site)', 'Memorial Amphitheater, used for state funerals and holiday ceremonies (Official Site)', 'Arlington House (The Robert E. Lee Memorial) (Official Site)', 'U.S. Marine Corps War Memorial (Iwo Jima Memorial) nearby (TripAdvisor)', 'Memorial Arboretum and its specimen trees (Official Site)']
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes as the grounds are expansive and hilly. Allow at least 4-5 hours for a thorough visit. Respectful behavior is required as this is a sacred military site. Security screening is required at the Welcome Center (HI Travel Tales).
attractions.nearby: U.S. Marine Corps War Memorial, Pentagon Memorial, Air Force Memorial, Arlington House (TripAdvisor).
